title: Time-Traveling Teen Lands in 1955

title: Monopoly: The Game That Took Over
story: Grab your top hat and race to Park Place! On November 5, 1935, Parker Brothers unleashed Monopoly upon the world. This property-trading game quickly became a family favorite, with players wheeling and dealing their way to riches. title: Nat King Cole Makes TV History
story: Smooth-voiced singer Nat King Cole broke barriers in 1956. On November 5, his variety show premiered on NBC. It was the first time an African-American hosted such a program on national television. Cole's talent and charm won over viewers, paving the way for more diverse faces on TV screens across America.
activity_question: What kind of show did Nat King Cole host?
bonus_fact: Nat King Cole was also a talented jazz pianist before becoming a famous singer.

title: Beach Boys Make a Splash Again
story: Surf's up! The Beach Boys caught a new wave of success in 1988. Their catchy tune 'Kokomo' from the movie 'Cocktail' soared to the top of the charts on November 5. It was a big comeback for the band, marking their first hit in about two decades. To this day, 'Kokomo' remains their most recent chart-topper.
activity_question: What movie was the song 'Kokomo' featured in?
bonus_fact: The Beach Boys are famous for their harmonies and songs about surfing, cars, and summer fun.

title: A Voice That Bridged Generations
story: Soaring vocals filled the air as Art Garfunkel entered the world in New York City in 1941. Born on November 5, this future music legend would become half of the iconic duo Simon and Garfunkel. Their song 'Bridge Over Troubled Water' touched hearts worldwide. Garfunkel's talents extended beyond music, as he also pursued acting in films like 'Carnal Knowledge'.
activity_question: What famous song is mentioned in the story?
follow_up_question: Besides singing, what other talent did Art Garfunkel have?
